emails.subject: Carolina Power & Light
emails.body: Carolina Power Light David: CP&L has marked up a confirm that we sent to them for a deal that was done 5/8/2001 to make it subject to their 5/18/98 Tariff agreement. We are in the process of negotiating an EEI Master with them but have some outstanding credit issues. How should I respond to their mark-up? This was one of Janice's files. Carol St. Clair EB 3889 713-853-3989 (Phone) 713-646-3393 (Fax) carol.st.clair@enron.com

emails.subject: Carolina Power & Light
emails.body: Carolina Power Light David: CP&L has marked up a confirm that we sent to them for a deal that was done 5/8/2001 to make it subject to their 5/18/98 Tariff agreement. We are in the process of negotiating an EEI Master with them but have some outstanding credit issues. How should I respond to their mark-up? This was one of Janice's files. Carol St. Clair EB 3889 713-853-3989 (Phone) 713-646-3393 (Fax) carol.st.clair@enron.com

emails.subject: Re: Carolina Power & Light
emails.body: Re: Carolina Power Light [I'll see you at approx. 1 p.m. today right?] Re the below, contact the credit group to see if they can speedily resolve the credit issues so as to bring the EEI to bear on the transansaction, clearly the best result. Credit will also indicate whether the size of the trade is such that they want to insist the trade is done under a completed EEI Master, rather than being documented the 'old way' and then brought under the EEI Master upon its completion. If the trade will be completed under the Tariff, you'll want to look at the Tariff to see what underlying terms you're dealing with (e.g., 1 year limit on term of trade completed under the Tariff, availability of LDs for a trade involving LD energy, etc.) If not already known to you, Genia or Elizabeth may have input on where things stood in Janice's negotiations with CP&L. Carol St Clair 05/22/2001 12:13 PM
